items.title: Mutation testing for the agentic era
items.summary: Code coverage is one of the most dangerous quality metrics in software testing. Many developers fail to realize that code coverage lies by omission: it measures execution, not verification. Test suites with high coverage can obfuscate the fact that critical functionality is untes

items.title: Try our new dimensional analysis Claude plugin
items.summary: We’re releasing a new Claude plugin for developing and auditing code that implements dimensional analysis, a technique we explored in our most recent blog post. Most LLM-based security skills ask the model to find bugs. Our new dimensional-analysis plugin for Claude Code takes a

items.title: Spotting issues in DeFi with dimensional analysis
items.summary: Using dimensional analysis, you can categorically rule out a whole category of logic and arithmetic bugs that plague DeFi formulas. No code changes required, just better reasoning! One of the first lessons in physics is learning to think in terms of dimensions. Physicists can oft

items.title: Code is ‘functional’ free speech under the First Amendment: Coin Center
items.summary: Crypto software developers are concerned about whether they could be held criminally liable for publishing their software, following high-profile convictions last year.

items.title: Bitcoin faces near-term pressure as liquidity tightens, Hilbert Group CIO says
items.summary: Russell Thompson warned of a sharp tightening in global liquidity that could weigh on risk assets and bitcoin in the near term, before expected U.S. policy action provides relief.

items.title: Global stablecoin rulemaking slows, prompting BIS to urge cooperation to avoid fragmentation risks
items.summary: To mitigate risks like sudden withdrawals, policymakers are debating safeguards such as limiting interest payments and offering issuers access to central bank backstops.

items.title: Bitcoin, ether, solana slide, oil jumps on renewed U.S.-Iran war risks
items.summary: Bitcoin traded at $74,335 after Iran reimposed controls on the Strait of Hormuz over the weekend, with the modest 1.6% pullback standing out against a 5.7% jump in Brent and a 1.2% drop in European equity futures.

items.title: RaveDAO's RAVE token risks another 50% crash amid price manipulation claims
items.summary: RAVE is trending inside a descending channel pattern, and is about to test the lower trendline target near $0.30 amid price manipulation concerns.

items.title: BIS warns dollar stablecoins could strain banks and policy
items.summary: BIS general manager Pablo Hernández de Cos said US dollar stablecoins may pose risks to financial stability and urges stronger global coordination on regulation.

items.title: Arbitrum freezes $71 million in ether tied to Kelp DAO exploit
items.summary: The layer-2 network's security council moved 30,766 ETH to a frozen intermediary wallet, accessible only via further governance action.

items.title: Bitcoin reclaims $75,000 as Iran ceasefire talks advance, equities rally resumes
items.summary: Bitcoin traded at $75,733 on Tuesday morning, up 1.5% over 24 hours, as Iran signaled it will send a team to Pakistan talks and Brent crude slipped ahead of the Wednesday ceasefire deadline.

items.title: Ripple wants the XRP Ledger to be quantum-proof by 2028. Here is its plan
items.summary: Ripple has laid out a four-phase plan to make the XRP Ledger quantum-resistant by 2028.

items.title: Inside the 'fake police raid' that forced a $1M Bitcoin transfer
items.summary: A fake police raid enabled a $1 million Bitcoin robbery, exposing the rise of wrench attacks and the shift from digital hacks to physical crypto threats.

items.title: Singapore’s OCBC launches tokenized gold fund on Ethereum and Solana
items.summary: The value of tokenized real-world assets on public blockchains is estimated at more than $29 billion, up more than 10% in the last 30 days.

items.title: Arbitrum freezes $71M of Ether connected to Kelp exploit
items.summary: Griff Green, a member of Arbitrum’s security council, said the group acted with input from law enforcement and “did not make this decision lightly."

items.title: US senator urges delay of CLARITY Act Senate markup until May: Report
items.summary: US Senator Thom Tillis said members of the crypto and banking industries still need more time to be heard.

items.title: Coinbase’s AI payments protocol x402 launches app store for AI agents
items.summary: Coinbase’s x402 AI payments protocol has launched Agentic.market, a platform where AI agents can discover and use AI-friendly services.

items.title: NY lawmaker proposes ‘AI dividend’ to address potential job losses
items.summary: The proposed AI dividend would be funded by taxes on AI use and equity stakes in AI companies, paying US citizens if automation meaningfully displaces workers.

items.title: Aave risk manager models 2 bad debt scenarios from Kelp DAO exploit
items.summary: The first scenario is far cheaper but runs the risk of rsETH depegging an estimated 15%, while the second is costlier but better protects Ethereum mainnet and concentrates losses at the layer 2 level.

items.title: North Korea’s crypto heist playbook is expanding and DeFi keeps getting hit
items.summary: More than $500 million was siphoned across the Drift and Kelp exploits in just over two weeks. What once looked like isolated breaches now resembles a sustained campaign, likely driven by the financial needs of a sanctioned state.
